title: The Frozen Pass
description: A blizzard traps you on Greymount Pass with night falling fast. Every choice could mean warmth or a cold grave.
author: AI

intro.passage: You are halfway through Greymount Pass when the blizzard strikes without warning. Snow drives sideways and the trail vanishes beneath your boots. The wind cuts through your cloak like iron. Above, a rocky ridge climbs into the storm. Below, a valley drops into shadow. Nearby, cliffs might hide a cave.

choices.label: climb toward the ridge
choices.hint: Ice crunches under your boots as you begin the steep ascent.

choices.label: descend into the valley
choices.hint: Loose stones shift beneath the snow as you start downward.

choices.label: search the cliffs
choices.hint: You press your hand along the rock face, feeling for an opening.

ridge-climb.passage: The ridge is narrow and the wind tries to throw you from it with every step. Ice coats the stone beneath your feet. Through the swirling snow you make out two shapes ahead. To the left, dark walls of a ruined hut. To the right, the ridge slopes down toward a line of black pines.

choices.label: investigate the ruin
choices.hint: The broken walls look sturdy enough to block the worst of the wind.

choices.label: head toward the pines
choices.hint: The dark tree line promises shelter from the biting wind.

stone-shelter.passage: The ruin is a shepherd's hut with three walls still standing and half a roof. It blocks the worst of the wind. Inside you find a dead fire pit, dry kindling stacked in a corner, and a frozen corpse sitting against the far wall. Snow has dusted the body's shoulders. A leather satchel rests in its lap.

choices.label: open the satchel
choices.hint: The leather is stiff with frost. You pry the buckle open carefully.

choices.label: light a fire and rest
choices.hint: The kindling is dry enough. Your fingers fumble with the flint.

marked-trail.passage: The satchel holds a hand-drawn map, a brass compass, and dried meat. The map marks a village called Frosthollow on the northern slope. A note in shaky script reads: do not take the valley, the river ice is thin. You pocket the map. Outside, cold wind tugs at the hut's broken walls.

choices.label: follow the map north
choices.hint: The compass needle swings north and holds steady in your palm.

choices.label: light a fire first
choices.hint: Night is falling and the cold is getting worse by the minute.

shelter-fire.passage: You coax the kindling into flame. The fire pushes back the cold and throws dancing shadows across the stone. Hours pass. The storm weakens near midnight and through a gap in the clouds you see stars. The dead traveler watches with empty eyes. You could move now while the wind is low.

choices.label: set out under the stars
choices.hint: The wind has dropped. This may be your only window to move.

choices.label: search the satchel
choices.hint: The dead traveler's bag might hold something useful for the road.

night-march.passage: Stars give just enough light to pick your way along the ridge. The air is brutal without the storm to trap any warmth. Your breath freezes before your face and your fingers go numb inside your gloves. After an hour the ridge opens onto a wide snowfield. The wind returns, harder than before.

choices.label: push across the snowfield
choices.hint: The open ground stretches ahead, white and featureless in starlight.

choices.label: burrow into the snow
choices.hint: Your legs are failing. The snow looks almost inviting.

valley-descent.passage: You half-slide, half-climb down the frozen slope. Loose rocks shift beneath the snow and your footing is poor. At the bottom a frozen river cuts across the valley floor. The ice gleams dull white in the fading light. Upstream, a dark shape spans the water. It could be a bridge.

choices.label: cross the frozen river
choices.hint: The ice looks thick enough, but you cannot be sure from here.

choices.label: head upstream to the bridge
choices.hint: The dark shape is hard to make out through the blowing snow.

frozen-river.passage: You step onto the ice. It groans beneath your weight but holds. Halfway across, a deep crack splits the air. A dark line races across the surface toward you. The far bank is thirty paces away. Behind you the ice is splitting into gray slabs. There is no time to think.

choices.label: sprint for the far bank
choices.hint: Your boots slip and scrape as you throw yourself forward.

choices.label: turn back to shore
choices.hint: The ice behind you is already breaking into dark water.

far-bank.passage: You throw yourself forward as the ice splits behind you. Your boots hit solid ground and you tumble into deep snow on the far bank. The river groans and shifts but you are across. Through the storm you see dark shapes of buildings ahead. No smoke rises from any chimney.

choices.label: enter the village
choices.hint: The buildings are dark and silent. Snow has drifted against every door.

choices.label: follow the riverbank north
choices.hint: A faint trail runs along the bank, half-buried in fresh snow.

abandoned-village.passage: The village is empty. Doors hang open and snow has drifted into every building. In the largest house you find a cold hearth, broken furniture, and a cellar stocked with sealed jars of food. Whatever drove these people away, they left in haste. The walls are solid and the roof still holds.

choices.label: barricade the door and stay
choices.hint: The cellar has enough food to last weeks if you are careful.

choices.label: search for a path onward
choices.hint: Staying means waiting. The mountain may have other plans for you.

hidden-path.passage: Behind the last house you find boot prints leading north into the trees. Someone passed through here not long ago. You follow the tracks up a winding trail through snow-laden pines. The prints are steady. After a mile the trees thin and you step onto an open snowfield high on the mountain's shoulder.

choices.label: cross the snowfield
choices.hint: The wind hits you hard as you leave the shelter of the trees.

choices.label: return to the village
choices.hint: The tracks could belong to anyone. You are not sure you trust them.

riverbank.passage: You follow the frozen river upstream, keeping to the rocky bank. The going is slow but the ground is solid beneath your feet. After half a mile the dark shape turns out to be a wooden bridge, old but intact. Snow sits heavy on its rails. Beyond it a path leads up into the trees.

choices.label: cross the bridge
choices.hint: The planks groan under your weight but the timber holds firm.

choices.label: keep following the river
choices.hint: The bank narrows ahead but the footing stays solid enough.

bridge-crossing.passage: The old bridge creaks but holds your weight. On the far side the path climbs through dense pines. You smell wood smoke before you see the cabin. It sits in a clearing, built from heavy logs, with a thin stream of smoke rising from the chimney. Firelight flickers behind a shuttered window.

choices.label: knock on the door
choices.hint: You hear movement inside. Someone is home.

choices.label: circle around the cabin
choices.hint: You are not sure who lives here. Better to look before you knock.

woodsman-cabin.passage: A broad woman opens the door with an axe in hand. She studies you for a moment, then steps aside. Inside is warm and spare. A fire crackles in the stone hearth. She offers broth without a word. In the morning, she says, there are two ways forward. Over the mountain or through the winter here.

choices.label: take the mountain path
choices.hint: She points north through the window. The peak is barely visible.

choices.label: stay and wait out winter
choices.hint: The warmth of the fire is hard to leave. Your bones ache.

cave-mouth.passage: You find a narrow crack in the cliff face just wide enough to squeeze through. Inside, the cave opens into a wider chamber. The air is warmer here and the wind cannot reach you. But you are not alone. Fresh tracks mark the dusty floor and the smell of animal musk hangs thick in the dark.

choices.label: follow the tracks deeper
choices.hint: The prints are large, with claw marks. Something big lives here.

choices.label: make camp near the entrance
choices.hint: The entrance is narrow enough to defend if anything comes.

wolf-den.passage: The tracks lead to a wide chamber littered with bones. Three wolves lie curled together for warmth. The largest raises its head and fixes yellow eyes on you. A low growl fills the space. Behind the pack you notice a second exit, a narrow crack leading to cold air and pale moonlight.

choices.label: back away slowly
choices.hint: You keep your eyes down and step backward without a sound.

choices.label: slip past toward the crack
choices.hint: The exit is close. If you move fast enough you might make it.

wolf-standoff.passage: The wolves spring to their feet. The largest bares its teeth and lunges. You swing your pack at its head and scramble for the crack. Teeth catch your cloak and tear it away. You squeeze through the gap and tumble into cold moonlight on a steep slope. Wind bites your skin where the cloak was torn. The pack snarls behind you.

choices.label: follow the slope down
choices.hint: The slope is steep but the snow is firm enough to walk on.

choices.label: search for another cave
choices.hint: You need shelter. The torn cloak will not last the night.

cave-camp.passage: You build a small fire near the entrance using dried moss and flint from your pack. The warmth slowly returns feeling to your fingers. Outside the storm howls on. You hear movement deeper in the cave, claws scraping stone, but the fire keeps whatever lurks there at a distance. Sleep comes in fits.

choices.label: wait for dawn
choices.hint: You feed the fire through the night and keep your back to the wall.

choices.label: explore deeper with a torch
choices.hint: You wrap dried moss around a stick and light it from the fire.

cave-dawn.passage: You keep the fire burning through the night. Twice, yellow eyes appear at the edge of the light and twice they retreat. Dawn comes gray and quiet. The storm has passed. You step outside to a world buried in white. A column of smoke rises from beyond a wooded slope to the north.

choices.label: head toward the smoke
choices.hint: Smoke means people. You set off through the deep fresh snow.

choices.label: descend into the valley
choices.hint: The valley floor looks calmer now that the storm has broken.

mountain-slope.passage: The northern slope is steep but the snow is firm enough to walk on. You pick your way between boulders and frozen scrub. Below you can see dark pines and beyond them a wide snowfield. The smoke you spotted rises from somewhere past the tree line to the east. A cold wind pushes at your back.

choices.label: cut through the forest
choices.hint: The pines offer shelter from the wind that batters the open slope.

choices.label: traverse to the snowfield
choices.hint: The snowfield is exposed but it looks like the faster route.

dark-forest.passage: The pines grow thick and their branches catch most of the falling snow. The silence is sudden and heavy after the howling wind above. Animal tracks cross the forest floor in every direction. Deeper in, you spot a faint orange glow between the trunks. A campfire. Voices carry on the still air.

choices.label: approach the campfire
choices.hint: The voices are low and calm. They do not sound like a threat.

choices.label: skirt around and head north
choices.hint: You keep your distance and move through the trees in silence.

campfire-strangers.passage: Three figures huddle around a struggling fire. A woman in heavy furs looks up as you approach. Another fool caught in the pass, she says. Sit. We have broth. They are trappers heading south who know these woods well. They offer to guide you to the valley road come first light.

choices.label: share the fire tonight
choices.hint: The broth is thin but warm. You sit and let the heat soak in.

choices.label: ask directions and press on
choices.hint: The woman points north and tells you to follow the ridge line.

trapper-camp.passage: You share broth and stories through the night. The woman, Maren, tells you of a village called Frosthollow beyond the snowfield. Her friends argue about the fastest route while wind shakes the branches above. When dawn breaks pink through the trees, Maren stands and kicks snow over the coals. Time to move.

choices.label: travel with the trappers
choices.hint: Maren shoulders her pack and waves you forward without a word.

choices.label: go alone toward Frosthollow
choices.hint: You thank them and set off north before the sun clears the trees.

snowfield.passage: The snowfield stretches before you, flat and white and bare. The wind has died and the morning sun turns everything blinding. Each step sinks knee-deep into the powder. After what feels like hours the ground slopes downward. Through the glare you see rooftops below and the thin gray line of chimney smoke.

choices.label: make for the village
choices.hint: The rooftops grow clearer with each step. You smell wood smoke.

choices.label: rest before the descent
choices.hint: Your legs are shaking. The snow looks soft enough to sit in.

mountain-descent.passage: The path down is steep but clear. Snow crunches under your boots in the morning stillness. The rooftops grow larger with every step. You can hear the distant sound of an axe splitting wood and smell bread baking. A wooden sign half-buried in snow reads Frosthollow. The village gate stands open.

choices.label: enter the village
choices.hint: Warm light spills from the windows of a large building near the gate.

choices.label: circle the village walls
choices.hint: Something makes you pause. You want to see the lay of the land.

frosthollow-inn.passage: You push open the heavy inn door and warmth hits you like a wall. A fire roars in the stone hearth. The innkeeper looks up and nods. Mountain traveler, she says. Sit by the fire. First bowl is free for anyone who survives the pass. You sit. Your hands shake as they thaw.

ending.title: Warmth at Last
ending.flavor: The best thing about nearly freezing to death is the first bowl of hot stew afterward.

trapper-dawn.passage: Maren and her friends lead you down trails you never would have found alone. They move through the snow like it is flat ground. By midday you reach a crossroads with a weathered signpost. The trappers wave farewell and vanish back into the trees. The road south stretches clear and safe before you.

ending.title: Safe Passage
ending.flavor: Sometimes survival is not a battle but the kindness of strangers around a fire.

cabin-ending.passage: You seal the doors and feed the hearth with broken furniture. The food will last weeks if you are careful. The storm returns that night and does not stop for three days. You sit alone listening to the wind. When spring finally comes you walk out into a changed world, thinner and quieter.

ending.title: The Long Winter
ending.flavor: You survived, but the silence of those empty rooms stays with you.

frozen-death.passage: You dig a hollow in the snow and curl into it, telling yourself you will rest for just a moment. The cold wraps around you like a blanket. Your shivering stops and a strange warmth spreads through your limbs. The stars above are very beautiful. You close your eyes and do not open them again.

ending.title: The Cold Sleep
ending.flavor: The mountain keeps what it takes.

ice-death.passage: You turn back but the ice is already gone beneath you. The slab tilts and dark water surges up around your boots. The cold hits like a hammer blow, driving the air from your lungs. The current pulls you under the shelf. Above you the world becomes a dim gray light that fades to nothing.

ending.title: Beneath the Ice
ending.flavor: The river was never yours to cross.
